standard_notation: Z -> c cbar pole observables: R_c, A_FB^{0,c}, A_c
process_name: Z-pole charm-quark partial-width ratio and asymmetries
implementation_difficulty_reason: A live constraint would require new electroweak-pole observable handling, LEP/SLC heavy-flavour covariance data, and model-specific matching to Z c_L and Z c_R coupling shifts; the existing Delta F=2 and lepton-dipole paths do not cover it.

commands: rg -n "Z.?->.?c|Zcc|Z c|A_FB|Afb|R_c|Rc|A_c|Ac\\b|forward-backward|Z-pole|Z pole|delta_g.*c|gLc|gRc|cbar|c cbar" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n "R_c|A_FB\\^\\{0,c\\}|A_FB.*c|A_c|Zcc|Z c c|Z.?->.?c|delta_g.*c|gL.?c|gR.?c" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n "M_Z|m_Z|Z_MASS|mass.*Z|Z boson|Z-boson" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
findings: No R_c, A_FB^c, A_c, Zcc coupling-shift, or electroweak-pole likelihood implementation was found.
Process-specific searches returned unrelated R_chi hadronic helpers and bridge-matching class names, not charm Z-pole code.
Only generic Z-mass/QCD-reference support surfaced at the evidence lines above.

paper_era_reference.role: Baseline arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor reference requested by the catalog instructions.
Post-CFW RS electroweak-precision and Z-fermion-coupling interpretation.
Post-2008 SM theory update for Z partial widths and branching ratios.
Prospective future-collider context for improved heavy-quark Z-pole measurements.

status_history.notes: Fetched cited PDG/LEP-SLC/arXiv sources; Zcc pole-observable value claims match.

status_history.reason: Opus round 3 sign-off; full PKA -> WA -> CA cycle-1 (clean first pass) -> fact-check VERIFIED; T013 charm-asymmetry context covered under this T012 entry per DA-4 addendum disposition; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_003_index.md.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(R_c^0\), \(A_{\rm FB}^{0,c}\), and \(A_c\).
This entry covers the charm-quark Z-pole partial-width ratio and asymmetry
observables under process id T012.  Plan v1 lists \(R_c\) as T012 and the charm
asymmetries as T013; the dispatch instruction asks for the combined
\(Z\to c\bar c\) pole-observable package under T012.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical current values are taken from the PDG 2025 Z-boson listing
snapshot in
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/T012/pdg\_2025\_z\_boson\_charm.txt},
accessed on 2026-05-16 (\texttt{pdg\_2025\_z\_boson\_charm}).  The measured
values are
\[
R_c^0 = 0.1721\pm0.0030,\qquad
A_{\rm FB}^{0,c}=0.0707\pm0.0035,\qquad
A_c=0.670\pm0.027 .
\]
For \(A_{\rm FB}^{0,c}\), the PDG listing gives the Z-pole charge asymmetry
as \(7.07\pm0.35\%\); the dimensionless value above is the direct percent
conversion (\texttt{pdg\_2025\_z\_boson\_charm}).  The LEP/SLC
final-combination snapshot in
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/T012/lepslc\_2006\_z\_resonance\_charm.txt}
records the same three charm-observable central values and uncertainties
(\texttt{lepslc\_2006\_z\_resonance\_charm}).
sections.relevance:
These observables constrain flavor-diagonal shifts in the left- and
right-handed \(Zc\bar c\) couplings.  In anarchic warped models, the charm
coupling shifts are usually less severe than the \(Zb\bar b\) constraint
because the charm is not tied to the same custodial-protection problem as the
third-generation bottom doublet.  They are still useful as a consistency check
on up-sector fermion localizations, bulk multiplet assignments, and the same
gauge-boson mixing effects that generate flavor-changing \(Z\) couplings.  A
large shift in \(Zc\bar c\) would also feed the global Z-pole heavy-flavour fit
that correlates charm and bottom pseudo-observables.
sections.post_2008:
No new LEP/SLC-style experimental average supersedes the legacy Z-pole charm
combination, so the PDG value remains data-dominated by the final LEP/SLC
heavy-flavour fit.  Since arXiv:0804.1954 (\texttt{cfw\_2008\_rs\_flavor}),
the RS literature has made the model dependence of ordinary \(Zf\bar f\)
couplings more explicit; the Casagrande--Goertz--Haisch--Neubert--Pfoh setup
treats the standard \(Z\) couplings, flavor-changing \(Z\) effects, and
electroweak precision constraints in a unified warped-flavor framework
(\texttt{casagrande\_2008\_rs\_ewpt}).  On the Standard-Model side, the
fermionic electroweak two-loop calculation of Z partial widths and branching
ratios was completed in arXiv:1401.2447
(\texttt{freitas\_2014\_z\_widths}).  Prospective FCC-ee work, including
arXiv:2107.00616 (\texttt{alcaraz\_2021\_z\_lineshape\_fcc}), revisits how
\(R_c\) and \(A_{\rm FB}^{c}\) could be improved with modern heavy-flavour
tagging, but those studies are not current experimental inputs.
sections.validity:
This is a precision electroweak coupling constraint, not a flavor-changing
decay or a stand-alone RS flavor bound.  It is robust as a measured Z-pole
pseudo-observable, but the translation into a bound on a 5D model is
embedding-dependent: custodial symmetry, the representation of the up-sector
quarks, brane kinetic terms, and fermion localization choices all affect the
predicted \(\delta g_{Lc}\) and \(\delta g_{Rc}\).  \(R_c^0\) is mostly a width
constraint, while \(A_c\) and \(A_{\rm FB}^{0,c}\) carry chiral information and
depend on the electroweak-pole fit conventions.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO}.  The required greps found no implementation of \(R_c\), \(A_c\),
\(A_{\rm FB}^{0,c}\), a Zcc coupling-shift calculation, or a correlated
electroweak-pole likelihood in \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, or
\texttt{tests/}.  The only Z-related matches were generic \(M_Z\) support in
\texttt{qcd/running.py:3}, \texttt{qcd/constants.py:11}, and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/qcd\_running.py:100}; process-specific searches also
returned unrelated \(R_\chi\) hadronic helpers.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH}.  A live constraint would need a new electroweak-pole observable
module, LEP/SLC covariance handling for heavy-flavour pseudo-observables, and
model-specific matching from the warped spectrum to \(\delta g_{Lc}\) and
\(\delta g_{Rc}\).  This is not covered by the existing \(\Delta F=2\) operator
basis or the lepton-dipole path.

priority_rationale_short: Zcc pole data checks up-sector profiles below Zbb
priority_rationale_full: The Z -> c cbar pole observables constrain flavor-diagonal charm coupling shifts and help test up-sector localizations, representations, and gauge mixing. They are clean precision pseudo-observables, but the charm couplings are usually less stressed than Z b bbar in anarchic RS. This makes T012 a meaningful Z-pole consistency constraint, not a dominant mass-scale driver.
